testModifications.reasoning: No test files were modified by the implementer. All 20 loader tests passed without modification.

criteria.criterion: src/prompts/templates/ contains test-writer.ts, implementer.ts, verifier.ts, single-session.ts — each exports defaultTemplate string
src/prompts/loader.ts exports loadOverride(role: PromptRole, workdir: string, config: NaxConfig): Promise<string | null>
Loader resolves path relative to workdir correctly
Loader returns null when config.prompts absent, role key absent, or file missing
Loader throws with clear message on unreadable file (permissions error)
NaxConfig gains optional prompts?: { overrides?: Partial<Record<PromptRole, string>> }
Unit tests cover all required scenarios (null cases, file exists, permission error)

issues.description: Node.js fs.existsSync usage violated Bun-native-only convention
issues.fix: Replaced fs.existsSync with Bun.file().exists() for Bun API compliance

issues.description: Biome formatter issue with function signature line wrapping
issues.fix: Collapsed loadOverride function signature onto single line

fixes.description: Use Bun-native APIs: replaced Node.js fs.existsSync with Bun.file().exists()
fixes.detail: Changed from import { existsSync } from 'node:fs' to using Bun.file(absolutePath).exists() for consistency with project conventions

fixes.description: Fixed Biome formatting: collapsed function signature to single line
fixes.detail: Reformatted loadOverride function signature to comply with formatter expectations

reasoning: All acceptance criteria are fully met. The implementation correctly loads prompt overrides relative to workdir, returns null for missing/absent configurations, throws descriptive errors on permission issues, and exports default template stubs. All 102 tests pass (20 loader-specific tests confirm core functionality). Two quality issues were identified and fixed: replacing Node.js fs.existsSync with Bun.file().exists() for Bun-native compliance, and correcting Biome formatting. No test assertions were loosened. The implementation is clean, follows conventions, and ready for integration.
